SEARCH

Class HashTable<T extends Comparable<T>>

java.lang.Object
HashTable<T>
Type Parameters:
T -
public class HashTable<T extends Comparable<T>> extends Object
A hash table implementation using chaining with GenericLinkedLists. This class is designed to store dictionary words and their number of occurrences, the assignment wanted a load factor of 0.6 and an array with a prime size in the form of 4k+3.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    HashTable(int estimatedCount)
    Constructs a hash table with a capacity based on a 0.6 load factor and 4k+3 prime.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(T element)
    Inserts an element into the table using the bucket's sorted add method.
    boolean
    contains(T element)
    Checks if the specified element exists within its corresponding hash bucket.
    double
    getActualLoadFactor()
    Calculates the current load factor based on unique entries and table capacity.
    GenericLinkedList<T>[]
    getBuckets()
    Provides access to the underlying bucket array for iteration purposes.
    int
    getCapacity()
    Returns the current physical capacity of the hash table's array.
    int
    getFrequency(T element)
    Returns the number of times a specific word has been inserted into the table.
    int
    getUniqueCount()
    Returns the total number of unique elements currently stored in the table.
    boolean
    remove(T element)
    Removes an element from the table and updates the unique word count if successful.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • HashTable

      public HashTable(int estimatedCount)
      Constructs a hash table with a capacity based on a 0.6 load factor and 4k+3 prime.
      Parameters:
      estimatedCount - The total number of words expected from the file.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public void add(T element)
      Inserts an element into the table using the bucket's sorted add method.
      Parameters:
      element - The data to be added to the hash table.

    • contains

      public boolean contains(T element)
      Checks if the specified element exists within its corresponding hash bucket.
      Parameters:
      element - The data element to search for.
      Returns:
      True if the element is found, false otherwise.

    • remove

      public boolean remove(T element)
      Removes an element from the table and updates the unique word count if successful.
      Parameters:
      element - The data element to be removed.
      Returns:
      True if the element was removed, false if it was not found.

    • getFrequency

      public int getFrequency(T element)
      Returns the number of times a specific word has been inserted into the table.
      Parameters:
      element - The word to look up.
      Returns:
      The frequency count of the word.

    • getCapacity

      public int getCapacity()
      Returns the current physical capacity of the hash table's array.
      Returns:
      The total number of buckets available.

    • getUniqueCount

      public int getUniqueCount()
      Returns the total number of unique elements currently stored in the table.
      Returns:
      The count of unique words.

    • getActualLoadFactor

      public double getActualLoadFactor()
      Calculates the current load factor based on unique entries and table capacity.
      Returns:
      The ratio of unique entries to the number of buckets.

    • getBuckets

      public GenericLinkedList<T>[] getBuckets()
      Provides access to the underlying bucket array for iteration purposes.
      Returns:
      The array of GenericLinkedList buckets.
